Claude works best when it can see the
instead of guessing from a description. Referencing files reduces prompt noise, preserves code context, and makes it easier to ask for precise edits across related files.
The most common pattern is to mention a file path with

You can also reference specific line ranges when you only want Claude to focus on part of a file. This is especially useful when:
- A change spans several related files.
- You want an explanation of a single module.
- You need Claude to modify code in a known location.
- You want to keep prompts short and avoid copying code manually.
A good habit is to group related files together and describe how they relate, rather than listing them in isolation.

Claude Code can also work with visual inputs such as screenshots, mockups, and design assets. Typical ways to attach them include:
-

This is useful for:
- UI bug reports.
- Design-to-code work.
- Comparing before/after screenshots.
- Debugging visual regressions.

If you reuse certain files often, add them to your project memory file so Claude can keep them in mind across sessions. For images, include only the most relevant screenshots rather than a large batch of unrelated visuals, so the session stays focused.

. Instead of asking Claude to reconstruct the situation from memory, you show the actual files or screenshots you want it to reason about.
